preamble.title: cardanofoundation/kyc-whitelist
preamble.description: KYC Whitelisting smart contracts for Cardano stablecoins

cardano/address/Address.title: Address
cardano/address/Address.description:
A Cardano `Address` typically holding one or two credential references.

 Note that legacy bootstrap addresses (a.k.a. 'Byron addresses') are
 completely excluded from Plutus contexts. Thus, from an on-chain
 perspective only exists addresses of type 00, 01, ..., 07 as detailed
 in [CIP-0019 :: Shelley Addresses](https://github.com/cardano-foundation/CIPs/tree/master/CIP-0019/#shelley-addresses).

cardano/address/Credential.title: Credential
cardano/address/Credential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/address/PaymentCredential.title: PaymentCredential
cardano/address/PaymentCredential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/address/StakeCredential.title: StakeCredential
cardano/address/StakeCredential.description:
Represent a type of object that can be represented either inline (by hash)
 or via a reference (i.e. a pointer to an on-chain location).

 This is mainly use for capturing pointers to a stake credential
 registration certificate in the case of so-called pointer addresses.

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output

kyc/types/Role.title: Role
kyc/types/Role.description:
Trust / privilege level.
 Serialises as a Plutus Constr with no fields:
   User          → Constr 0 []
   Institutional → Constr 1 []
   VLei          → Constr 2 []

kyc/types/TENode.title: TENode
kyc/types/TENode.description: Node datum for the Trusted Entity List.

fields.title: vkey
fields.description: Raw 32-byte Ed25519 verification key (sort key is blake2b_224(vkey))

kyc/types/TERedeemer.title: TERedeemer
kyc/types/TERedeemer.description: Redeemer for the Trusted Entity validator

anyOf.title: TEInit
anyOf.description: Initialise the list (mint head node).  Tx must be signed by the bootstrap issuer.

anyOf.title: TEAdd
anyOf.description: Add a new trusted entity.  Covering node must be provided for insertion order.

anyOf.title: TERemove
anyOf.description: Remove a trusted entity.  Covering node must be provided.

kyc/types/WLConfig.title: WLConfig
kyc/types/WLConfig.description:
Config for the KYC Whitelist validator.
 The `oref` is consumed exactly once in WLInit – prevents duplicate whitelists.

kyc/types/WLNode.title: WLNode
kyc/types/WLNode.description: Node datum for the KYC Whitelist.

fields.title: pkh
fields.description: The user's payment key hash (sort key)

fields.title: active
fields.description: Whether this user is currently allowed to transact

fields.title: role
fields.description: Trust level assigned to this user

kyc/types/WLRedeemer.title: WLRedeemer
kyc/types/WLRedeemer.description: Redeemer for the KYC Whitelist validator

anyOf.title: WLInit
anyOf.description: Initialise the list.  Tx must be signed by a key present in the TEL.

anyOf.title: WLAdd
anyOf.description:
Add user to the whitelist.
 Requirements:
   1. Tx is signed by `new_pkh` (user self-registers)
   2. `entity_endorsement` is the 64-byte Ed25519 signature of `new_pkh` bytes
      produced off-chain by the trusted entity's private key.
   3. `endorsing_entity_vkey` is the corresponding 32-byte raw Ed25519 public key.
      Its blake2b_224 hash must match a TEL member visible in reference inputs.
   4. `new_role` must be ≤ the endorsing entity's role in the TEL (enforced by `role_lte`).

 This allows the trusted entity to sign off-chain and hand the user a
 "credential" they can use to add themselves without any further co-operation.

fields.title: new_role
fields.description: Role to assign to the new user

fields.title: endorsing_entity_vkey
fields.description: Raw 32-byte Ed25519 verification key of the trusted entity

fields.title: entity_endorsement
fields.description: 64-byte Ed25519 signature of `new_pkh` bytes by the trusted entity

anyOf.title: WLSetActive
anyOf.description:
Set the active flag on an existing node.
 Only a trusted entity (present in TEL reference inputs) may do this.
